reasoning: Direct consequence of the fully verified `lemma_set_of_first_n_nat_is_finite`. Correct by inductive proof.
`Set::new(|i: nat| i < 0)` has no members, so it equals `Set::empty()`. Correct by definition of `Set::new`.
`n-1 < n` is trivially true, so `Set::new(|i: nat| i < n).contains(n-1)` holds by definition. Correct.
`n < n` is false, so `n` is not in the set. Correct by definition.
The extensional equality follows from `i < n || i == n <==> i < n + 1` for naturals. Correct set identity.
